title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 5 (Fragment 5)
text:
Old Niccolo had his look of a baby about to cry. Aunt Francesca said, “We’re not asking this personally—”

“But, my dear,” said Chrestomanci, “don’t you see that I can only do something like this as a personal matter? Please ask me personally. I shan’t let the strict terms of my mission interfere with what I owe my friends.” He smiled then, and his eyes swept around everyone gathered at the great table, very affectionately. He did not seem to exclude Marco. “So,” he said, “I think my best plan for the moment is to go on to Rome. I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information, which should enable me to pin down this enchanter. At the moment, all we know is that he exists. If I’m lucky, I can prove whether Florence, or Siena, or Pisa is paying him—in which case, they and he can be indicted at the Court of Europe. And if, while I’m at it, I can get Rome, or Naples, to move on Caprona’s behalf, be very sure I shall do it.”

“Thank you,” said Old Niccolo.

For the rest of supper, they discussed how Chrestomanci could best get to Rome. He would have to go by sea. It seemed that the last stretch of border, between Caprona and Siena, was now closed.

Much later that night, when Paolo and Tonino were on their way to bed, they saw lights in the Scriptorium. They tiptoed along to investigate. Chrestomanci was there with Antonio, Rinaldo and Aunt Francesca, going through spells in the big red books. Everyone was speaking in mutters, but they heard Chrestomanci say, “This is a sound combination, but it’ll need new words.” And on another page, “Get Elizabeth to put this in English, as a surprise factor.” And again, “Ignore the tune. The only tune which is going to be any use to you at the moment is the Angel . He can’t block that.”

“Why just those three?” Tonino whispered.

“They’re best at making new spells,” Paolo whispered back. “We need new war-spells. It sounds as if the other enchanter knows the old ones.”

They crept to bed with an excited, urgent feeling, and neither of them found it easy to sleep.

Chrestomanci left the next morning before the children went to school. Benvenuto and Old Niccolo escorted him to the gate, one on either side, and the entire Casa gathered to wave him off. Things felt both flat and worrying once he was gone. That day, there was a great deal of talk of war at school. The teachers whispered together. Two had left, to join the Reserves. Rumors went around the classes. Someone told Tonino that war would be declared next Sunday, so that it would be a Holy War. Someone else told Paolo that all the Reserves had been issued with two left boots, so that they would not be able to fight. There was no truth in these things. It was just that everyone now knew that war was coming.

The boys hurried home, anxious for some real news. As usual, Benvenuto leaped off his waterbutt. While Tonino was enjoying Benvenuto’s undivided attention again, Elizabeth called from the gallery, “Tonino! Someone’s sent you a parcel.”

Tonino and Benvenuto sprang for the gallery stairs, highly excited. Tonino had never had a parcel before. But before he got anywhere near it, he was seized on by Aunt Maria, Rosa and Uncle Lorenzo. They seized on all the children who could write and hurried them to the dining room. This had been set up as another Scriptorium. By each chair was a special pen, a bottle of red war-ink and a pile of strips of paper. There the children were kept busy fully two hours, copying the same war-charm, again and again. Tonino had never been so frustrated in his life. He did not even know what shape his parcel was. He was not the only one to feel frustrated.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows fondness or tenderness.
vocabulary.example: He smiled then, and his eyes swept around everyone gathered at the great table, very affectionately.

vocabulary.definition: A line separating two political or geographical areas.
vocabulary.example: The last stretch of border between Caprona and Siena was now closed.

vocabulary.definition: A joining or merging of different parts or qualities in which the component elements are individually distinct.
vocabulary.example: This is a sound combination, but it’ll need new words.

vocabulary.definition: A person who uses magic spells; a wizard or magician.
vocabulary.example: ...this enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: Very enthusiastic and eager about something.
vocabulary.example: They crept to bed with an excited, urgent feeling...

vocabulary.definition: Feeling upset or annoyed because of inability to change or achieve something.
vocabulary.example: Tonino had never been so frustrated in his life.

vocabulary.definition: Treating all rivals or disputants equally; fair and just.
vocabulary.example: I know certain quarters there, where I can get impartial information...

vocabulary.definition: Formally accuse someone of a serious crime.
vocabulary.example: They and he can be indicted at the Court of Europe.

vocabulary.definition: To get involved in a situation where you are not wanted or needed, often causing a problem.
vocabulary.example: The strict terms of my mission interfere with what I owe my friends.

vocabulary.definition: Formally supplied or distributed something.
vocabulary.example: All the Reserves had been issued with two left boots.

vocabulary.definition: An important assignment carried out for political, religious, or other purposes.
vocabulary.example: ...the strict terms of my mission interfere...

vocabulary.definition: To speak quietly and indistinctly, often complaining or expressing dissatisfaction.
vocabulary.example: Everyone was speaking in mutters.

vocabulary.definition: A package or bundle of something sent by mail or delivered.
vocabulary.example: Tonino had never had a parcel before.

vocabulary.definition: A situation or issue that concerns an individual's private affairs, not official business.
vocabulary.example: I can only do something like this as a personal matter.

vocabulary.definition: To identify or locate something precisely; to find exact information about something.
vocabulary.example: ...which should enable me to pin down this enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: Areas or districts in a town or city; sometimes used to mean particular parts of a place.
vocabulary.example: I know certain quarters there...

vocabulary.definition: Military forces not in active service but available for mobilisation if needed.
vocabulary.example: Two had left, to join the Reserves.

translations.ru: скрипторий (место переписывания книг в средние века)

vocabulary.definition: A room in medieval monasteries devoted to the copying of manuscripts; here likely used metaphorically as a study or writing room.
vocabulary.example: They saw lights in the Scriptorium.

vocabulary.definition: To grab hold of or take sudden advantage of something or someone.
vocabulary.example: He was seized on by Aunt Maria, Rosa and Uncle Lorenzo.

vocabulary.definition: A continuous area or expanse of land or water.
vocabulary.example: The last stretch of border was now closed.

vocabulary.definition: Precise and exact conditions or rules that must be followed.
vocabulary.example: The strict terms of my mission...

vocabulary.definition: To walk quietly and carefully on one's toes.
vocabulary.example: They tiptoed along to investigate.

vocabulary.definition: Requiring immediate action or attention.
vocabulary.example: They crept to bed with an excited, urgent feeling...

vocabulary.definition: Magic spells intended for use in warfare or battle.
vocabulary.example: We need new war-spells.

vocabulary.definition: To signal goodbye by waving one's hand as someone leaves.
vocabulary.example: The entire Casa gathered to wave him off.
